mentorship.calibration_recommendations: 1) Sample 200 postings stratified by year (2024/2025/2026) and seniority (junior/mid/senior/staff+); hand-label as mentorship-positive/negative. Compute precision/recall per concept and per keyword to identify weak terms. 2) Pay special attention to 'guide', 'coach', 'lead', 'review', 'onboarding', 'pairing', 'champion' — each has a high false-positive base rate. Implement the proximity guards in `exclusions` before counting. 3) Compute baseline density at junior level: mentorship markers should be near-zero in IC-junior postings and rise sharply at senior/staff/principal. If baseline is non-trivial, guards are too loose. 4) Consider a two-pass approach: Pass 1 unrestricted match -> Pass 2 apply exclusion guards. Report both for sensitivity analysis. 5) Track Tier-1 'Direct mentorship language' (mentor*) as the gold-signal anchor; other concepts should correlate with it within senior cells. If they do not, re-examine guards. 6) For overlap with People-management and Verification, build a co-occurrence matrix and report disjoint vs joint counts so the reader can see how decisions about boundary terms (code review, raise the bar) affect headline numbers.
mentorship.notes: Boundary calls: (a) 'Code review' — listed here because the brief includes 'code reading' and the dominant senior-IC framing is teaching; cross-listed with Verification with explicit guidance to count by surrounding feedback/learning vocabulary. (b) 'Architecture review' — included here per the brief; in flat-org / staff+ JDs this is the canonical mentorship-via-influence activity. (c) 'Tech lead' / 'technical leadership' — kept here (influence, not headcount); People-management list should keep 'engineering manager', 'people manager', 'direct reports', 'manage a team of N'. (d) 'Coaching' — strong false-positive risk from benefits language; require object-targeting at engineers/team. (e) Track 'mentor*' separately as the cleanest Tier-1 anchor for trend reporting. (f) For the 2024->2026 AI-transition story, expect 'pair programming'/'mob programming' density to potentially decline (LLM-as-pair-partner substitution) while 'code reading'/'code review'/'architecture review' may rise (humans verify and teach over machine output) — pre-register these as hypotheses so the vocabulary choice is not post-hoc. (g) British/American: 'mentor*', 'coach*', 'review*', 'onboard*', 'program(me)' — include 'programme'/'programmes' variants where compound forms occur ('mentorship programme', 'onboarding programme'); add to the variant list before final run.

performance.definition: Captures JD language demanding low-level performance work (profiling, latency/throughput tuning, memory/CPU optimization), systems-internals depth (OS, kernel, networking, distributed-systems internals, hardware-aware programming), and explicit depth-claim framings ('deep understanding of X', 'expert-level', 'from first principles'). Distinct from high-level architecture/orchestration: this is the internals/low-level end where employers demand expertise that AI tooling cannot easily substitute.

core_concepts.name: performance_optimization_general
core_concepts.definition: General references to performance work, tuning, and optimization as an explicit job activity.
core_concepts.regex_notes: Use word boundaries on 'perf' to avoid 'perfect/perform/perfume'; require it to appear in compound forms like 'perf tuning', 'perf work', 'perf engineering' or as standalone token followed by domain word.

redefine_proposal.issue: 'high performance' / 'high-performance' fire on culture-deck and team-naming boilerplate ('high-performance team', 'high-performance, diverse thought') as much as on actual high-performance computing.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Keep precise variants 'high-performance computing' and 'HPC' only; drop bare 'high performance' / 'high-performance' OR guard them by requiring co-occurrence with 'computing', 'system', 'application', 'database', 'code' within +/- 5 tokens (excluding 'team', 'culture', 'environment').

core_concepts.name: profiling_and_benchmarking
core_concepts.definition: Mention of profilers, benchmarking, tracing, and instrumentation used to diagnose performance issues.
core_concepts.regex_notes: 'profile' alone is ambiguous (LinkedIn profile, user profile) — only count compound forms or '-ing/-er' suffixes in tech contexts.

redefine_proposal.issue: 'instrumentation' (222 hits) and 'tracing' (179 hits) carry strong false-positive rates from unrelated domains (industrial instrumentation, requirements tracing). 'profiling' (345) has moderate data-profiling contamination.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Drop 'instrumentation' as a bare keyword (replace with 'code instrumentation', 'binary instrumentation', or 'performance instrumentation'). Drop 'tracing' as a bare keyword (replace with 'distributed tracing', 'CPU tracing', 'execution tracing' — note 'distributed tracing' is missing from the list and would be a clean signal). Guard 'profiling' to exclude 'data profiling'.

core_concepts.name: latency_throughput_scale
core_concepts.definition: Explicit metrics/objectives around latency, throughput, QPS, p99, and high-scale traffic.
core_concepts.regex_notes: 'real time' is broad — keep but flag overlap with streaming/data-engineering vocab.

redefine_proposal.issue: 'real-time' (1492 hits) and 'high volume'/'high-volume' (315 hits combined) overwhelmingly capture streaming-data and generic-volume framing rather than latency/throughput perf objectives. The concept's hit_rate (13.5%) is largely driven by these noisy strings.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Drop bare 'real-time'/'realtime' or guard them with an RTOS/RT-systems requirement. Drop 'high volume' (with space) and guard 'high-volume' to require co-occurrence with traffic/transactions. The concept's signal should rely on the metric-named keywords (latency, throughput, p99, QPS, RPS, sub-millisecond) which are far cleaner.

core_concepts.name: low_level_systems_programming
core_concepts.definition: Low-level programming work: assembly, C/C++ systems, memory management, SIMD/vectorization, lock-free data structures.

redefine_proposal.issue: The concept conflates two labor markets: (1) low-level performance/systems work (assembly, SIMD, memory model, lock-free) which is the topic's intent, and (2) embedded/firmware engineering (medical devices, avionics, automotive) which is its own category and brings in 1500+ postings dominating the concept.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Either split into two concepts ('low_level_systems_programming' for SIMD/atomics/memory-model/assembly + 'embedded_firmware' separate), or drop 'firmware', 'embedded systems', 'embedded software', 'systems engineer', 'systems engineering' from this concept and rely on the precise low-level keywords. Also remove the literal-collision keywords 'assembly', 'SSE'.

core_concepts.name: os_kernel_internals
core_concepts.definition: Deep operating-system and kernel internals knowledge.

core_concepts.name: networking_internals
core_concepts.definition: Deep networking-stack knowledge: TCP/IP internals, kernel bypass, RDMA, congestion control.

redefine_proposal.issue: BGP (40 hits) is the single largest contributor but it is a routing-protocol skill; the concept's actual internals signal lives in DPDK/RDMA/InfiniBand/XDP/eBPF/kernel-bypass — all with very low hit rates (1-10).
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Demote or drop bare 'BGP'; the existing low-volume kernel-bypass keywords are the true signal. Add VPP, SPDK, P4 (see ADDS) to broaden coverage of the dataplane-internals slice that genuinely exists in the data.

core_concepts.name: concurrency_parallelism
core_concepts.definition: Concurrency, parallelism, and multi-threading at a depth beyond standard async/await usage.

redefine_proposal.issue: Two keywords ('channels', 'fiber') are essentially false-positive only in this corpus — they map to marketing/storage/optical contexts, not concurrency. They inflate the concept hit_rate without providing real concurrency signal.

core_concepts.name: distributed_systems_internals
core_concepts.definition: Internals-level distributed systems work: consensus, replication protocols, consistency models, partitioning.

redefine_proposal.issue: Generic terms ('consensus', 'replication', 'partitioning') fire heavily on non-internals usage (stakeholder consensus, ETL replication, table partitioning). Meanwhile half the keywords are zero-hit theoretical-CS terms (linearizability, CAP theorem, vector clocks, CRDT singular form, MVCC, LSM tree).
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: For 'consensus' and 'replication', require neighboring algorithmic context ('Raft', 'Paxos', 'log', 'leader', 'quorum') OR drop them. Drop or merge zero-hit theoretical terms: keep the few that do fire ('CRDTs', 'consistency models', 'two-phase commit', 'WAL') and consider that this concept is a low-recall by-design signal.

core_concepts.name: hardware_aware_programming
core_concepts.definition: Hardware-aware programming: GPU/CUDA, FPGAs, CPU microarchitecture, accelerator-specific optimization.

redefine_proposal.issue: 'Metal' has catastrophic literal-match collision (sheet metal, bare metal hosting). 'RTL' collides with React Testing Library. 'HIP' is an acronym fragile. The concept also splits awkwardly between GPU programming (CUDA, cuDNN, ROCm) and FPGA/ASIC hardware design (Verilog, VHDL).
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Drop 'Metal', 'RTL', 'HIP'. Consider splitting into 'gpu_programming' (CUDA, GPU kernels, cuDNN, Triton, Vulkan, Metal-API guarded) and 'hardware_design_internals' (FPGA, ASIC, Verilog, SystemVerilog, VHDL, microarchitecture, cache hierarchy, NUMA).

core_concepts.name: compilers_and_runtimes
core_concepts.definition: Compiler internals, JITs, garbage collection, runtime tuning.
core_concepts.regex_notes: 'GC' alone is ambiguous (could mean General Counsel, Google Cloud) — require GC tuning/pauses/collector context or co-occurrence with JVM/heap.

redefine_proposal.issue: Bare 'compiler' / 'compilers' / 'code generation' have shifted in meaning: 'compiler' often means 'C++ compiler flags' toolchain mention, and 'code generation' now frequently means 'GitHub Copilot AI code generation'.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Drop bare 'compiler', 'compilers', 'code generation'. Rely on 'compiler internals', 'compiler optimization(s)', 'LLVM', 'MLIR', 'JIT compilation', 'AOT compilation', 'codegen' (which in the data appears mainly in backend-compiler context), 'operator fusion', 'optimization passes', 'JVM tuning', 'JVM internals'.

core_concepts.name: database_storage_internals
core_concepts.definition: Database engine internals, query optimization, storage engine work.

redefine_proposal.issue: Iceberg/compaction/execution-plan are data-engineering surface-area, not engine-internals. The concept overlaps heavily with data-warehouse tooling.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Drop 'Iceberg', 'compaction', 'execution plan' from this concept (they belong to a data-engineering concept if needed). Keep 'database internals', 'storage engine(s)', 'query optimizer', 'PostgreSQL internals', 'MySQL internals', 'InnoDB' for the internals signal even though hit-counts are low.

core_concepts.name: depth_claim_language
core_concepts.definition: Explicit demand-for-depth phrasings employers use to signal expert-level / first-principles understanding.
core_concepts.regex_notes: These are template phrases — recommend pairing with a technical noun within ~5 tokens (e.g., 'deep understanding of distributed systems') to avoid generic 'deep understanding of our customers'. Capture both the phrase and a windowed-cooccurrence variant if possible.

redefine_proposal.issue: Concept currently fires on 28.5% of postings driven almost entirely by generic boilerplate ('strong understanding of', 'solid understanding of', 'deep understanding of') applied to trivial domain skills (HTML5, OOP, CI/CD). This makes the concept synonymous with 'is a job posting' rather than measuring depth/internals-of-X demand. The intended construct (employer signaling first-principles, expert-level technical depth) is being drowned out.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Either (a) drop this concept entirely from the perf topic and convert it into a topic-agnostic modifier that boosts a posting's score only when the depth phrase is followed by an internals-domain noun (kernel, runtime, JVM, compiler, distributed systems, GPU, memory, latency, networking stack, etc.), or (b) keep only the high-specificity phrases ('first-principles', 'from first principles', 'under the hood', 'intimately familiar with', 'intimate knowledge of', 'rigorous understanding') and drop the high-volume generic ones.

core_concepts.name: scaling_and_efficiency
core_concepts.definition: Scaling-axis work and efficiency framings: scaling to N, cost/efficiency, resource utilization.
core_concepts.regex_notes: 'scalability' is broad — keep, but note this concept overlaps with orchestration/architecture topic; treat overlap as additive rather than miscategorization.

redefine_proposal.issue: Mixes three different concepts: (1) very-large-scale operational claims ('massive scale', 'scalable systems', 'scale to millions'), (2) perf-resource-efficiency ('memory footprint', 'memory pressure', 'CPU utilization'), and (3) FinOps/cost ('cost optimization', 'cost efficiency', 'capacity planning'). FinOps is a distinct topic from internals/perf and currently dominates concept hits.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Split: keep 'scaling axis / massive-scale' as one concept; promote 'memory efficiency / footprint / pressure / leak / utilization' into the perf-optimization or low-level-systems concept; remove FinOps terms ('cost optimization', 'cost efficiency', 'capacity planning') from this topic — they belong in a separate cloud/operations topic.

core_concepts.name: algorithmic_optimization
core_concepts.definition: Algorithm- and data-structure-level optimization, complexity analysis.

redefine_proposal.issue: 'data structures' is universal hiring-bar language with no algorithmic-optimization signal. Half of the concept's keywords are zero-hit (Big O variants, asymptotic complexity).
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Drop 'data structures'. Keep specific complexity language ('time complexity', 'space complexity', 'computational complexity', 'complexity analysis', 'algorithm optimization', 'numerical optimization', 'numerical methods'). Consider that this concept's true positive rate is small; it may be best merged into performance_optimization_general.

performance.calibration_recommendations: 1) Run a sample (n=200) of high-match postings and manually classify each as technical-perf vs HR-perf vs business-perf; the depth_claim_language and performance_optimization_general concepts will need the most filtering. 2) Implement co-occurrence guards for ambiguous tokens ('deep understanding of', 'real time', 'profile', 'GC', 'OS', 'scale') by requiring a technical noun within a 5-10 token window. 3) Treat depth_claim_language as a *modifier* concept: report it both raw and as 'depth_claim near technical noun' to separate fluff from genuine depth demand. 4) Track per-concept density (occurrences / 1000 tokens) rather than binary presence; this concept area will show most signal in the *intensity* dimension over 2024-2026. 5) Cross-check overlap with the Orchestration vocabulary: 'distributed systems' appears in both — decide whether to dual-count or assign primary topic by adjacent tokens (internals/consensus/replication => this list; architecture/design/microservices => Orchestration). 6) Validate hardware-aware terms (CUDA, FPGA, GPU) against role-title metadata — they spike in ML/infra roles and may need normalization across role cells.
performance.notes: This list emphasizes the *internals / low-level* end of the SWE skill spectrum, where the hypothesis is that AI substitution is weakest and JD demand may rise during 2024-2026. Three concepts (performance_optimization_general, depth_claim_language, scaling_and_efficiency) have the highest false-positive risk and should be reported with both raw counts and guarded counts. Compound forms ('performance-critical', 'low-latency') are listed both hyphenated and unhyphenated to handle inconsistent JD typography. Acronym-heavy concepts (CUDA, RDMA, DPDK, eBPF) are reliable signals — case-insensitive match recommended but consider preserving case-sensitive match for short acronyms that collide (BPF, GC, OS, SME). Depth-claim phrases are template-like; a windowed co-occurrence regex ('(deep|strong|expert|in-depth|thorough)\W+(understanding|knowledge|expertise|grasp|familiarity)\W+of\W+(\w+\W+){0,5}(<technical noun>)') will outperform raw-phrase matching.

process_scaffolding.definition: Vocabulary capturing traditional software-process, SDLC, and project-management language used in job descriptions: agile/scrum/kanban/waterfall methodologies, sprint and ceremony cadence, requirements engineering, specification authoring, V&V, project coordination, scheduling, milestones, change management, and governance. This is the classical process-and-methodology layer that orchestrates human teams building software, distinct from agentic-AI task orchestration and from correctness-gating verification.

core_concepts.name: Agile methodology
core_concepts.definition: Generic agile framing language: agile principles, agile delivery, agile mindset, agile transformation, agile coaching.
core_concepts.regex_notes: Use \bagile\b to avoid 'fragile'/'fragility'. Treat 'agile/scrum' and 'agile-scrum' as the same token — strip punctuation before matching.

core_concepts.name: Scrum framework
core_concepts.definition: Scrum-specific roles, ceremonies, and artifacts.
core_concepts.regex_notes: 'SM' and 'PO' are dangerous abbreviations; only count when adjacent to scrum-context tokens or expansion already matched. 'Scrum' alone is safe (no common collision).

redefine_proposal.issue: Two-letter acronym keywords (SM, PO) collide with unrelated SAP/Oracle/AWS terminology and add hundreds of false positives across the broader topic; CSM collides with ServiceNow Customer Service Management which is a strong false-positive cluster.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Drop 'SM' and 'PO' outright. For 'CSM', require co-occurrence with 'scrum'/'agile'/'certified'/'master' in same sentence; otherwise the ServiceNow CSM module dominates.

core_concepts.name: Sprints and iterations
core_concepts.definition: Time-boxed iteration vocabulary and story-point estimation.
core_concepts.regex_notes: 'sprint' is largely safe in JD context; avoid only if a fitness/athletics context is suspected (rare in SWE JDs).

redefine_proposal.issue: 'velocity', 'iteration', 'iterative', 'epic' all overfire on non-Agile senses (developer velocity as productivity metric, design iteration, Epic Systems healthcare, iterative culture phrasing). The concept is meant to capture time-boxed Scrum/SAFe iteration vocabulary, not generic 'we iterate quickly' culture.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Restrict to phrase-level: 'sprint*', 'sprint planning/cycle/goal', 'iteration planning', 'team velocity', 'sprint velocity', 'story points', 'user stor*', 'epics' (plural only — singular 'epic' too noisy), 'backlog grooming/refinement', and add 'blockers' / 'DSU' / 'refinement'. Drop bare 'epic', and require co-occurrence guards on bare 'velocity', 'iteration', 'iterative'.

core_concepts.name: Kanban / Lean / Waterfall and other methodologies
core_concepts.definition: Non-scrum process frameworks frequently named alongside agile.
core_concepts.regex_notes: 'lean' alone is fine; 'XP' is risky (Windows XP, experience points) — require nearby 'extreme programming' or 'agile'/'scrum' context. 'V-model' and 'V model' need word boundaries to avoid 'V model number'.

redefine_proposal.issue: Two of the highest-hit keywords in the concept ('SAFe' 557, 'LeSS' 567) are essentially noise from case-insensitive matching of 'safe' and 'less'. Without case-sensitive matching, the concept's apparent prevalence is fictitious.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Either enforce case-sensitive matching for these two acronyms (with token boundaries), or require strict co-occurrence guards. Note that 'SAFe' also belongs more naturally in 'Agile methodology' / 'Scrum framework' since it's a scaling framework — consider moving and deduping ('SAFe' already appears under both Agile methodology and this concept).

core_concepts.name: Requirements engineering
core_concepts.definition: Eliciting, analysing, and documenting product/system requirements.
core_concepts.regex_notes: Match 'requirement(s)?' as base form. 'NFR' is safe in technical context; 'DoD' could collide with 'Department of Defense' — require nearby 'definition'/'done' or other agile token.

redefine_proposal.issue: The concept is dominated by the bare token 'requirements' (49% hit rate) which is largely a JD section header, not requirements-engineering practice. The signal-to-noise ratio is poor enough that this concept will appear universal across SWE postings even when no real RE work is described.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Drop bare 'requirements' and 'requirement' as scoring tokens (or strip the JD-header occurrence in preprocessing). Anchor the concept on action-verb phrases ('gather requirements', 'gathering requirements', 'requirements analysis', 'requirements engineering', 'requirements management', 'requirements traceability', 'translate requirements', 'capture requirements', 'elicit requirements') and tool/artifact names ('DOORS', 'JAMA', 'Polarion', 'BRD', 'SRD', 'NFR', 'RTM', 'traceability matrix', 'use cases'). This preserves the concept's intent without the tautology.

core_concepts.name: Specification authoring
core_concepts.definition: Writing technical, functional, design, or product specs.
core_concepts.regex_notes: 'spec' alone is risky (could be 'spectrum', 'special', 'specific'); require word boundary and avoid prefix matches. Use \bspec(s|ification|ifications|ifying|ify|ced|ed)?\b. 'RFC' could match RFC HTTP standards — usually still process-context-relevant.

redefine_proposal.issue: The concept uses verb 'specify'/'specifying' (generic 'configure'/'set' verb in DB and config contexts) and bare nouns 'spec'/'specs' which are ambiguous (SpecFlow tool, 'spec out' as informal verb). The high-precision content sits in the qualified phrases (technical/functional/design/product specifications) and acronyms (SRS, ERD, RFCs, ADRs).
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Drop or guard 'specify'/'specifying'; demote bare 'spec'/'specs' to a soft signal requiring nearby 'design|review|out|technical|functional|product'. Add 'ADRs' (already evidenced) as a clean abbreviation alongside the existing 'architecture decision records'.

core_concepts.name: Verification & Validation (V&V)
core_concepts.definition: Process-level verification and validation activities (distinct from low-level test verification).
core_concepts.regex_notes: 'V&V' should be matched literal (with optional spaces around &). Standalone 'verification'/'validation' will overlap with the Verification topic — recommend reserving the V&V acronym + 'verification and validation' phrasing for this concept and giving the bare verbs to the Verification topic.

redefine_proposal.issue: The concept conflates process-level V&V (the topic definition's intent: formal qualification testing, IV&V, design/system verification & validation, V-model gates) with low-level data/model/test verification that pervades ML, ETL, and QA postings. Bare verbs 'verify', 'validate', 'validating', 'validated' supply the bulk of hits and consist almost entirely of non-process matches.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Drop the bare verb family ('verify','verifies','verifying','validate','validates','validating','validated'). Anchor the concept on the multi-word V&V phrases already in the list ('verification and validation', 'validation and verification', 'V&V', 'IV&V', 'qualification testing', 'design verification', 'system validation', etc.) plus the new 'ASPICE'. Treat single-word 'verification'/'validation' as supporting evidence requiring co-occurrence with 'plan|protocol|design|system|process|qualification' within a small window.

core_concepts.name: SDLC and process governance
core_concepts.definition: Lifecycle, governance, change-management, and process-improvement vocabulary.
core_concepts.regex_notes: 'CR' is too generic — only count if other change-management tokens nearby. 'audit'/'audits' might also appear in security context; tolerate overlap.

redefine_proposal.issue: 'ART' (515 hits) and 'PLC' (110 hits) are three-letter tokens whose dominant meanings ('state of the art' and 'Programmable Logic Controller') are unrelated to process governance and inflate the concept by ~600 postings.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Drop both. The intended SAFe Agile Release Train meaning is already captured by 'agile release train' / 'release train' / 'release trains'. The Product Life Cycle meaning is already captured by 'product lifecycle' / 'product life cycle'.

core_concepts.name: Project coordination
core_concepts.definition: Coordinating people, work, and dependencies across teams.
core_concepts.regex_notes: 'orchestrate' overlaps with the Orchestration topic — restrict to 'orchestrate teams'/'orchestrating teams' here; leave bare 'orchestrate' to the agentic-AI topic.

redefine_proposal.issue: Verb stems 'coordinate', 'facilitate', 'liaise' and their inflections produce 1.5k+ hits each but the snippets show overwhelmingly generic SWE collaboration usage ('coordinate with project leaders', 'facilitate cross-functional collaboration') that any modern engineering JD will contain. The concept currently functions as a SWE-baseline rather than a process-scaffolding marker.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Replace bare verb forms with phrase-level markers that require an object: 'cross-functional coordination', 'cross-team coordination', 'stakeholder coordination', 'project coordination', 'dependency management', 'stakeholder alignment' (already present). Demote the bare verbs to a 'soft signal' tier counted only when they co-occur with 'sprint|stakeholder|cross-team|dependencies|release' within +/-30 chars.

core_concepts.name: Scheduling and milestones
core_concepts.definition: Time-boxed planning, milestones, deadlines, roadmaps, and delivery commitments.
core_concepts.regex_notes: 'schedule' is broad — include only when in project/delivery context if precision is critical, otherwise tolerate.

core_concepts.name: Project / program management roles & tooling
core_concepts.definition: PM roles, certifications, and ticket-tracking tools that index process scaffolding.
core_concepts.regex_notes: 'PM' has many false positives ('a.m./p.m.', 'private message', 'particulate matter', 'PMP'); guard with surrounding tokens. 'Linear' is risky (linear regression / linear algebra) — only count when surrounded by tooling context (Jira, Asana, etc.) or capitalised in product-name form.

redefine_proposal.issue: Two- and three-letter role acronyms (PM, TPM, TPMs) carry severe semantic collisions (clock time, Trusted Platform Module, Technical Performance Measures) and 'Linear' as a tool name collides with 'linear algebra' which dominates DS/ML postings.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Drop 'PM', 'TPM', 'TPMs', 'Linear'. Spelled-out forms ('project manager', 'project managers', 'technical program manager', 'technical program managers', 'PMs') already cover the role meanings cleanly.

exclusions.guard: Restrict to occurrences within a window of 'requirements'/'system'/'design'/'qualification'/'validation' to capture process-V&V usage.

exclusions.reason: 1345 hits but a substantial share are 'data governance', 'cloud governance', 'AI/LLM model governance', 'API governance' rather than software-process governance per se.
exclusions.guard: Likely keep (process governance is a fuzzy umbrella) but be aware that 'data governance' is the dominant compound and may overlap with the data-platform topic. Consider co-occurrence with 'process'/'change'/'release'/'compliance'/'audit' for stricter scoping.

exclusions.concept: Kanban / Lean / Waterfall and other methodologies
exclusions.reason: Case-insensitive matching catches the common adjective 'safe' in phrases like 'safe operation', 'safe deployment practices', 'safe and maintainable factory'. All five visible examples are false positives; true SAFe-as-Scaled-Agile-Framework usage is buried.
exclusions.guard: Require case-sensitive 'SAFe' as a whole token, OR require co-occurrence with 'agile|scaled|framework|train|certification|agilist' within +/-30 chars.

exclusions.concept: Kanban / Lean / Waterfall and other methodologies

exclusions.guard: Require case-sensitive 'LeSS' (mixed-case as defined by Large-Scale Scrum) AND co-occurrence with 'scrum|scaled|agile|framework' within +/-50 chars; without a guard this keyword is unusable.

exclusions.reason: 5,793 hits is plausibly correct for SWE postings, but the bare token also matches 'fast-paced, agile environment' — generic adjectival usage that conflates a culture buzzword with methodology adoption. Many snippets pair 'agile' with no real process content.
exclusions.guard: Keep, but flag 'agile' alone as a weak signal; require co-occurrence with one of {scrum, kanban, sprint, ceremonies, methodology, framework, SAFe, lean} for high-confidence process-scaffolding scoring.

exclusions.concept: Kanban / Lean / Waterfall and other methodologies
exclusions.reason: Even after dropping bare 'lean' for the methodology sense, downstream consumers should note 'lean DevOps', 'lean startup environment' are culture, and 'Lean/Six Sigma' is quality methodology not process methodology.
exclusions.guard: Replace bare 'lean' with phrase-level keywords only: 'lean software development', 'lean principles', 'lean startup', 'lean methodology'.

exclusions.guard: Keep, but for spec-authoring evidence prefer the qualified phrases (technical/functional/design/system/product specifications) which are already in the list. Treat bare 'specifications' as weak signal.

exclusions.reason: Hits include 'specify identifiers of the database', 'specify all attributes' — generic 'configure'/'set' usage rather than authoring a specification document.
exclusions.guard: Require co-occurrence with 'requirement|design|spec|document|architecture' within +/-30 chars.

exclusions.reason: 1,692 hits — top examples include 'DB Validations', 'Tableau Dashboard validation', 'Reviews and validates requirements' — heavy mixture of data/model validation (not process V&V) and V&V proper.
exclusions.guard: Require co-occurrence with 'verification|qualification|design|system|process|plan|protocol' within +/-30 chars to bias toward process V&V; or only count when paired with 'verification'.

exclusions.guard: Strip JD section-header occurrences (line starts with 'Requirements:' or 'Requirements\n'); count only when followed/preceded by an action verb {gather, capture, elicit, analyze, document, translate, manage, trace, refine}.

exclusions.guard: Require co-occurrence with 'gather|capture|elicit|document|business|functional|system|user|technical|analysis|specification|trace' within +/-30 chars.

exclusions.reason: Case-sensitive name of IBM Rational DOORS tool but 'DOORS' uppercase could also appear as emphatic plural; visible examples are clean (all DOORS=IBM tool). Low risk but single-syllable noise possible.

exclusions.reason: Three-letter token also appears in 'BDD Spec flow/Cucumber framework' (a tool name, SpecFlow), and 'spec' as informal verb for 'design'. Low impact but ambiguous.

process_scaffolding.calibration_recommendations: 1) Run a 200-posting random-sample audit per quarter and flag postings where ambiguous tokens (PM, PO, SM, DoD, XP, spec, lean, retro) fire without their disambiguating context — measure precision per token; drop or re-guard tokens scoring <0.85 precision. 2) Build a co-occurrence guard: count an ambiguous abbreviation only if at least one unambiguous process-scaffolding token (e.g., agile, scrum, sprint, kanban, jira) appears in the same posting. 3) Pre-strip punctuation around '/' '&' '-' so 'agile/scrum', 'agile-scrum', 'V&V', 'V & V', 'change-management' all normalise. 4) Lowercase before matching except for acronyms PM/PO/SM/DoD/XP/ART/CR/PMP/PMO/TPM/SDLC/CMMI/ITIL/SAFe — keep these case-sensitive to reduce false positives. 5) Track per-concept density (occurrences / token-count) AND per-posting binary presence; the binary measure is more robust to JD length variation. 6) Consider weighting Tier 1 concept counts equally rather than summing all variants — it neutralises vocabulary-bloat where 'scrum'-heavy JDs dominate just because they list every ceremony.
process_scaffolding.notes: Boundary calls: (a) bare 'verification'/'validation' is delegated to the separate Verification topic; this topic claims only the V&V/IV&V acronym and the explicit 'verification and validation' phrasing — flag this in the paper's methods section so the two topics don't double-count. (b) 'orchestrate' similarly delegated to the Orchestration topic except for explicit 'orchestrate teams'. (c) PM/program-management tooling (Jira, Asana, etc.) is included because it is a strong proxy for ticket-driven process scaffolding; if the paper prefers tooling to live in a separate 'tooling' topic, lift the eleventh concept out. (d) DevOps/CI-CD vocabulary is intentionally NOT included here — that belongs to a deployment-pipeline topic. (e) The paper's hypothesis (that this layer shrinks relative to orchestration as senior work shifts to agentic AI) means false negatives are costlier than false positives at the senior tier — recommend the looser Tier-2 keyword set for senior-cell measurements and the stricter guarded set for cross-cohort comparisons.

legacy_stack.definition: Technologies, platforms, and stacks that signal a legacy, pre-cloud-native, pre-LLM enterprise environment in software engineering job descriptions. These include mainframe languages, legacy Microsoft and Java EE stacks, deprecated database platforms, on-prem virtualization/identity tooling, legacy enterprise integration buses, and legacy desktop/client frameworks. The presence of these markers in a JD suggests maintenance of pre-2015-era enterprise systems rather than greenfield cloud-native or AI-era development.

core_concepts.name: Mainframe languages and platforms
core_concepts.definition: Languages and operating environments tied to IBM mainframe and midrange systems, typically used for legacy financial, insurance, and government workloads.
core_concepts.regex_notes: RPG must be guarded against role-playing-game and 'RPG' in gaming contexts. Use a co-mention rule (RPG within 100 chars of AS/400, IBM i, COBOL, iSeries, etc.). 'IBM i' is short and risky — require word boundary and 'IBM' prefix.

redefine_proposal.issue: Strong concept overall, but missing the most common mainframe utilities and schedulers that appear repeatedly in snippets (IDCAMS, SMP/E, SDSF, IMS, IMS DB, CA-7, Control-M, AutoSys, Micro Focus). 'Adabas' and 'Natural/Adabas' are duplicated — current 'Natural/Adabas' (1 hit) is a strict subset of 'Adabas' (3 hits).
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Add IMS, IMS DB, IDCAMS, SMP/E, SDSF, Micro Focus, CA-7, Control-M, AutoSys (latter three could optionally form a 'legacy batch scheduling' sub-cluster). Collapse 'Natural/Adabas' into 'Adabas' with a co-occurrence guard for {Natural, Software AG}.

core_concepts.name: Legacy general-purpose languages
core_concepts.definition: Older general-purpose languages whose presence in a modern JD typically implies maintenance of legacy code rather than new development.
core_concepts.regex_notes: Pascal is high-noise (matches names, 'Pascal case'); require co-mention with Delphi or 'Object Pascal'. VBA is moderately current in finance/Excel contexts — treat as ambiguous. Perl alone is borderline; only flag 'Perl 5' or co-mention with mainframe markers.

redefine_proposal.issue: VBA fires at 0.26% mostly on Excel-VBA in trading/finance JDs. Excel VBA is current ('developing in Excel VBA'), not a legacy-system marker. Python 2 / Perl 5 are correct legacy markers.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Either drop VBA or split into 'Excel VBA' (not legacy) vs 'Access VBA / classic VB6 conversion' (legacy). Consider adding ColdFusion/CFML to a separate concept since they're a distinct legacy web stack.

core_concepts.name: Legacy Microsoft .NET stack
core_concepts.definition: Pre-.NET Core era Microsoft web and application frameworks tied to Windows Server / IIS hosting and the .NET Framework 4.x runtime.
core_concepts.regex_notes: CRITICAL: '.NET' alone matches modern .NET 6/7/8 — DO NOT count bare '.NET' as legacy. Only count '.NET Framework' (with 'Framework' qualifier) or specific legacy sub-products. '.NET' requires custom regex because '.' is non-word boundary; use pattern like (?<![\w.])\.NET\b. ASP.NET MVC (without version) is borderline — only MVC 5 / older is clearly legacy.

redefine_proposal.issue: Bare '.NET Framework' fires on JDs that list both .NET Framework and .NET Core/.NET 6+ — this is a modern hybrid posture, not legacy. WebForms, WCF, VB.NET, Classic ASP, WPF, WinForms are clean legacy markers.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Drop bare '.NET Framework'. Require version-pinning ('.NET Framework 4.5/4.6/4.7/4.8') OR co-occurrence with at least one specific legacy-MS marker (WebForms, Classic ASP, VB.NET, WCF, ASMX) for legacy classification.

core_concepts.name: Legacy Microsoft server / collaboration
core_concepts.definition: On-prem Microsoft server products and collaboration suites that predate the cloud-first (Azure/M365) era.
core_concepts.regex_notes: Bare 'SharePoint' and 'Exchange' are too ambiguous (SharePoint Online is current; 'Exchange' is a common English word). Require version qualifier or 'on-prem'/'on premise' modifier. VSS is highly ambiguous (Volume Shadow Copy, vector space, etc.) — only count when near 'Visual SourceSafe' or other legacy MS markers.

redefine_proposal.issue: BizTalk and BizTalk Server are duplicated across this concept and 'Legacy enterprise integration / ESB / messaging'. SSIS/SSRS/SSAS (the SQL Server BI stack) are arguably more 'on-prem MS data tooling' than 'collaboration' — the concept name is misleading.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Pick one home for BizTalk (the ESB concept is a better fit). Rename this concept to 'Legacy on-prem Microsoft server stack' so SSIS/SSRS/SSAS/TFS fit naturally. Add Crystal Reports and Business Objects (or place those under ERPs).

core_concepts.name: Legacy Java EE / app server stack
core_concepts.definition: Pre-Spring-Boot, pre-microservices Java enterprise application servers and EE specifications associated with monolithic deployments.
core_concepts.regex_notes: WAS is highly ambiguous (English verb); only count when near 'WebSphere' or 'IBM'. JSP and Servlets are borderline — still appear in current JDs but increasingly legacy; flag as soft signal. 'Java 7' and earlier is clearly legacy; Java 8 is borderline (still widely supported).

core_concepts.name: Legacy enterprise SOAP / web services
core_concepts.definition: SOAP-based web service standards and WS-* protocols associated with pre-REST enterprise integration.
core_concepts.regex_notes: SOAP alone is borderline (still used in some integrations); strongest signal is co-mention with WSDL or 'SOAP web services'. XSD is too generic (XML schemas are current) — drop or require co-mention with SOAP.

redefine_proposal.issue: Concept overlaps internally with 'Legacy Java EE / app server stack' on JAX-WS and JAX-RPC (cross-list collisions confirm this). The concept is also small (45 postings, 0.23% rate) — it largely captures the same postings as Java EE.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Either keep JAX-WS/JAX-RPC only in the Java EE concept and remove from this one, or merge this concept into Java EE as 'Legacy Java EE / SOAP services'. Consider that 'SOAP web services' on its own (27 hits) is often a modern integration concern, not legacy — guard with co-occurrence to {WebSphere, WebLogic, JBoss, WS-*, BizTalk, MuleSoft, TIBCO}.

core_concepts.name: Legacy enterprise applications and ERPs
core_concepts.definition: On-prem ERP, CRM, and groupware suites that predate the SaaS / cloud-CRM era and typically require specialized legacy maintenance.
core_concepts.regex_notes: 'Domino' alone matches Domino's Pizza and DominoDB; require 'Lotus' or 'IBM' prefix or co-mention. 'World' (JDE World) is too generic on its own — require co-mention with JD Edwards. SAP S/4HANA is current; only legacy when 'R/3' or 'ECC' qualifier present. ABAP is still active but is a strong legacy-Microsoft-style signal.

core_concepts.name: Legacy database platforms
core_concepts.definition: Database engines and platforms associated with pre-cloud, on-prem deployments — particularly older mainframe and proprietary relational systems.
core_concepts.regex_notes: DB2, Teradata, Sybase, Informix are still in active use but are strong legacy-stack signals — count them. PL/SQL and T-SQL alone are NOT legacy (current); only count specific old SQL Server / Oracle versions. Oracle generic is current — only old version qualifiers count as legacy.

redefine_proposal.issue: Concept mixes truly-legacy DBs (Sybase, Informix, FoxPro, Paradox, DB2/400) with old versions of still-supported DBs (Oracle 10g/11g, SQL Server 2000-2012) and with PL/SQL — which is just 'Oracle DB' and is not by itself a legacy signal. PL/SQL alone is responsible for 41% of the concept's postings_hit (268/648), distorting the rate.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Split into two sub-concepts: (1) 'Truly legacy DB engines' (DB2 mainframe variants, Sybase, Informix, FoxPro, Paradox, OpenVMS Rdb) and (2) 'Aging-version markers' (Oracle 10g/11g, SQL Server 2000-2012). Move PL/SQL to a guarded 'Oracle ecosystem skill' that only counts when co-occurring with another legacy marker. Add IMS / IMS DB (mainframe hierarchical DB) here or in the mainframe concept.

core_concepts.name: Legacy virtualization and on-prem infrastructure
core_concepts.definition: On-prem hypervisors, virtualization suites, and remote-desktop platforms that predate or compete with public-cloud-native infrastructure.
core_concepts.regex_notes: 'Citrix' on its own is the company name; specifically 'Citrix XenApp/XenDesktop/Workspace' indicates the virtualization product. ESX matches in technical contexts but also possibly in chemistry abbreviations — keep word boundary. VMS alone is risky (matches 'CMS', 'LMS', etc.); prefer 'OpenVMS'.

core_concepts.name: Legacy identity and directory services
core_concepts.definition: On-prem identity, authentication, and directory services that predate cloud-native IAM (Okta, Azure AD/Entra, AWS IAM).
core_concepts.regex_notes: 'AD' alone is extremely high noise (Anno Domini, advertising, 'ad-hoc', 'add'); ALWAYS require 'Active Directory' spelled out, or 'AD' co-mentioned within 50 chars of 'LDAP', 'GPO', 'domain controller', or 'Kerberos'. LDAP and Kerberos are still in current use but are legacy-leaning signals. 'Azure AD' / 'Entra ID' are MODERN — exclude these from the pattern.

redefine_proposal.issue: Active Directory, LDAP, Kerberos, Group Policy are all current technologies in hybrid-cloud and security-clearance JDs. Treating them as legacy markers contaminates the topic with cloud-architect and modern-IAM roles.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Restrict the concept to clearly-deprecated identity products (Tivoli, SiteMinder, ADFS-on-prem). Move AD/LDAP/Kerberos/Group Policy to a 'hybrid-cloud / on-prem identity' topic, not 'legacy'.

core_concepts.name: Legacy enterprise integration / ESB / messaging
core_concepts.definition: Heavyweight enterprise service bus and message-broker products associated with pre-event-streaming (Kafka) integration patterns.
core_concepts.regex_notes: 'MQ' alone matches MQ-9 Reaper, McQuarrie, and other unrelated abbreviations — require 'IBM MQ', 'WebSphere MQ', or 'MQSeries' (or co-mention with IBM/messaging). MuleSoft is borderline-legacy (still actively sold but signals integration-heavy enterprise environment). SOA is borderline — pattern is older but term still used.

redefine_proposal.issue: Mixes truly legacy ESBs (TIBCO BW, webMethods, BizTalk, Oracle SOA Suite, Mule ESB community edition) with modern iPaaS/cloud (MuleSoft Anypoint, current Kafka-adjacent ESB framings) and with the architectural pattern 'SOA' itself.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Drop 'SOA' and 'Service Oriented Architecture' as keywords (they describe a pattern, not a legacy product). Move 'MuleSoft' to a separate 'integration platforms' topic. Keep TIBCO/webMethods/BizTalk/IBM MQ/MQSeries as legacy.

core_concepts.name: Legacy version control and build
core_concepts.definition: Pre-Git version control systems and pre-CI/CD build tools indicating a legacy SDLC.
core_concepts.regex_notes: Perforce is still actively used in gaming/large monorepos — borderline legacy. CVS is highly ambiguous (CVS pharmacy, curriculum vitae); require 'Concurrent Versions' or co-mention with version control terms. Make / Ant are technically legacy build tools but extremely common — flag as soft signal. Hudson is the pre-fork name of Jenkins; clearly legacy.

redefine_proposal.issue: Concept currently mixes (a) clearly pre-Git legacy SCM (ClearCase, PVCS, VSS, TFVC) with (b) tools that are not 'legacy' in the cloud-native sense — Perforce (still dominant in games/embedded) and GNU Make (still dominant in C/C++/Linux). Mixing inflates the concept hit rate and dilutes the legacy signal. Concept also lacks the most common pre-Git centralized VCS markers (Subversion/SVN/CVS).
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Remove GNU Make. Either remove Perforce or move it to a separate 'specialty / non-cloud SCM' bucket with a co-occurrence guard. Add Subversion, SVN, CVS as primary keywords. Rename the concept to 'Legacy / pre-Git centralized version control'.

exclusions.reason: Bare '.NET' matches BOTH legacy (.NET Framework 4.x) and modern (.NET 6/7/8, .NET Core). Modern .NET is current and dominant in 2024-2026 JDs.
exclusions.guard: Only count '.NET Framework' (with the word 'Framework') or specific legacy sub-products (WebForms, WCF, VB.NET, Classic ASP). Exclude '.NET Core', '.NET 5', '.NET 6', '.NET 7', '.NET 8', 'ASP.NET Core'. Use lookahead/lookbehind: \.NET(?!\s*(Core|[5-9]|\d{2})).

exclusions.reason: Two-letter token matches Anno Domini, advertising ('ad'), 'ad-hoc', 'add', and many other words.
exclusions.guard: ALWAYS require 'Active Directory' spelled in full, OR 'AD' within 50 characters of 'LDAP', 'GPO', 'domain controller', 'Kerberos', or 'forest/domain'. Never count bare 'AD'.

exclusions.reason: Azure AD (now Microsoft Entra ID) is a MODERN cloud identity service, not legacy on-prem AD.
exclusions.guard: Explicitly exclude 'Azure AD', 'AAD', 'Entra ID', 'Entra', 'Microsoft Entra' from the Active Directory legacy match.

exclusions.reason: Matches 'MQ-9 Reaper' (drone), McQuarrie, and various abbreviations.
exclusions.guard: Only count 'IBM MQ', 'WebSphere MQ', 'MQSeries', or 'MQ' co-mentioned within 50 chars of 'queue', 'broker', 'JMS', or 'IBM'.

exclusions.reason: 'Citrix' is the company name; only the virtualization products (XenApp, XenDesktop, Workspace, Virtual Apps and Desktops, Citrix Receiver) are the legacy-virt signal. Citrix Networks / Citrix ADC is a different product line.
exclusions.guard: Prefer 'Citrix XenApp', 'Citrix XenDesktop', 'Citrix Virtual Apps', 'Citrix Workspace'. Bare 'Citrix' counts but with lower weight.

exclusions.reason: Highly ambiguous: Volume Shadow Copy Service, Vector Space Search, various other meanings.
exclusions.guard: Only count when explicitly 'Visual SourceSafe' is spelled out, or 'VSS' co-mentioned with 'Visual SourceSafe', 'source control', or 'version control'.

exclusions.reason: Matches role-playing-game contexts, especially in gaming/entertainment JDs.
exclusions.guard: Only count 'RPG' when co-mentioned with 'AS/400', 'IBM i', 'iSeries', 'COBOL', 'mainframe', or as 'RPGLE'/'RPG IV'/'RPG/400'.

exclusions.reason: Common English past-tense verb 'was'.
exclusions.guard: Only count 'WAS' when co-mentioned with 'WebSphere' within 50 chars, or replace with 'WebSphere Application Server'.

exclusions.reason: CVS pharmacy is a major US employer; also matches 'curriculum vitae'.
exclusions.guard: Only count 'CVS' when co-mentioned with 'version control', 'Subversion', 'Git', 'source control', or spelled out as 'Concurrent Versions System'.

exclusions.reason: Matches Domino's Pizza, Project Domino, DominoDB (a different product), Domino Data Lab (modern ML platform).
exclusions.guard: Only count 'Domino' when co-mentioned with 'Lotus', 'IBM', or 'Notes'. Exclude 'Domino Data Lab'.

exclusions.reason: Matches the personal name Pascal, 'Pascal case' naming convention, and Blaise Pascal references.
exclusions.guard: Only count 'Pascal' when 'Object Pascal', 'Delphi', or 'Turbo Pascal' is co-mentioned. Exclude 'PascalCase'/'Pascal case'/'PascalCasing'.

exclusions.reason: Three-letter abbreviation has other meanings (e.g., 'Earnings Before...', 'Energy...').
exclusions.guard: Only count 'ESB' when 'Enterprise Service Bus' is spelled out OR co-mentioned with TIBCO/Mule/BizTalk/SOA.

exclusions.reason: Java 8 is still widely supported (LTS) — borderline legacy. Including it would over-count.
exclusions.guard: Exclude Java 8 from legacy markers; only count Java 7 and earlier (or 'JDK 1.6'/'JDK 1.7').

exclusions.reason: Make is universal in C/C++ and Linux ecosystems; Ant is still used but legacy-leaning. Including would over-count.
exclusions.guard: Treat as 'soft' legacy signal only if other legacy markers co-occur in the same JD.

exclusions.reason: SOAP integrations exist in current JDs (especially financial/healthcare); not always legacy.
exclusions.guard: Strongest signal is 'SOAP' + 'WSDL' co-mention or 'SOAP web services'. Treat bare 'SOAP' as soft signal.

exclusions.reason: Possible 3-letter collisions, but sampled snippets are clean ('Mvs Cobol', 'MVS JCL', 'CICS, MVS, ISPF'). One snippet 'MVS, VIP, RSI' is ambiguous.
exclusions.guard: Require co-occurrence with mainframe context tokens (z/OS, JCL, COBOL, CICS, ISPF, OS/390) within +/-50 chars to be safe; otherwise it's mostly fine.

exclusions.reason: 3-letter token. Sampled hits are all VMware ESX, but 'ESX' could match ESXi tail or proper-noun strings.
exclusions.guard: Require word boundary and either 'VMware' within +/-30 chars or membership in a hypervisor list; current data is clean but the surface area is risky.

exclusions.reason: Risk of matching as a substring (titles like 'Software Engineer I, AIX'); current sample is clean and refers to IBM AIX UNIX.
exclusions.guard: Word-boundary regex \bAIX\b only; keep.

exclusions.reason: Clean in sample; sometimes appears in 'Oracle Solaris (UNIX)' lists alongside Linux/Windows in security-clearance JDs as a legacy-friendly skill rather than current focus.
exclusions.guard: Keep as-is; high precision.

exclusions.reason: Clean; TFS is unambiguously Team Foundation Server. Note many snippets describe migrating away from TFS to Azure DevOps — which is itself a legacy-stack signal.
exclusions.guard: Keep; consider weighting 'migrating TFS' contexts as strong legacy-maintenance evidence.

exclusions.reason: Heavily co-occurs with 'Azure AD'/'Entra'/cloud IAM in modern hybrid-cloud JDs; not pre-cloud per se. Many snippets are cloud-architect roles integrating with on-prem AD.
exclusions.guard: Require absence of 'Azure AD'/'Entra'/'Okta' co-occurrence, OR require co-occurrence with 'on-prem' / 'Domain Controller' / 'Group Policy'. Otherwise demote weight.

exclusions.reason: Modern Ping Identity, ForgeRock, Hadoop/Cloudera, and security-engineer JDs mention LDAP as a current protocol; not legacy per se.
exclusions.guard: Demote to weak signal; require co-occurrence with mainframe/legacy-AD context for legacy classification.

exclusions.reason: Listed alongside SAML/OAuth/JWT/SSO in modern security-engineer JDs; current Kafka/Hadoop/SRE roles routinely require it.
exclusions.guard: Same as LDAP — demote, or require legacy co-context.

exclusions.reason: Common in current Windows-centric DoD/clearance JDs alongside cloud and modern PowerShell. Not specifically legacy.
exclusions.guard: Demote weight or require on-prem AD co-occurrence.

exclusions.reason: Hyper-V is current Microsoft hypervisor still shipping with Windows Server 2022/Azure Stack; not strictly legacy.
exclusions.guard: Treat as 'on-prem virtualization' rather than legacy; or require co-occurrence with VMware/ESX/Citrix.

exclusions.reason: 268 hits — Oracle PL/SQL is still widely used in current Oracle-cloud and on-prem Oracle DB jobs; appears in cloud-ready/Docker JDs.
exclusions.guard: Demote; require co-occurrence with another legacy marker (Forms, EBS, WebLogic, mainframe) or Oracle pre-12c.

exclusions.reason: Teradata Vantage is a current cloud DW; appears alongside Snowflake/Redshift/BigQuery in modern data-engineer JDs.
exclusions.guard: Demote; not a clean legacy marker without 'Teradata 13/14/15' context.

exclusions.reason: Mostly listed in 'Oracle, SQL Server, DB2, Teradata' enumerations in current data-warehouse/data-engineer JDs; not specifically legacy unless DB2 for z/OS.
exclusions.guard: Use 'DB2 for z/OS' / 'DB2/400' subvariants for high-precision legacy; keep 'DB2' but at lower weight.

exclusions.reason: Perforce Helix is current and dominant in game dev / large binary asset teams (Unity/UE5). Not a legacy SDLC signal in 2024.
exclusions.guard: Demote or move out of legacy; or require co-occurrence with non-game industry context.

exclusions.reason: One of 5 example snippets reads 'Spark, ADFS, Databricks, Azure Data Factory' where ADFS appears in a data-platform list, suggesting it may be misused for Azure Data Lake/Factory storage rather than AD Federation Services.
exclusions.guard: Require ADFS to co-occur with one of {Active Directory, AD, Federation, SSO, SAML, identity, authentication, Kerberos, Azure AD, Okta, MFA} within the same posting; otherwise discard.

exclusions.reason: Adabas is a real Software AG mainframe DB, but the bare token risks matching unrelated names. In the sample all 3 hits are valid; concern is forward-looking. Also 'Natural/Adabas' is a redundant separate keyword.
exclusions.guard: Require Adabas to co-occur with one of {Natural, Software AG, mainframe, JCL, COBOL, zOS, z/OS, DB2}. Consider folding 'Natural/Adabas' into the same regex (Adabas\W+|Natural\W*/?\W*Adabas).

exclusions.reason: ESB is a generic architecture acronym; many hits read 'Mule ESB' or 'SOA, ESB concepts' without committing to a heavyweight legacy product. Borderline rather than wrong, but risks inflating concept hit rate.
exclusions.guard: Either rename concept to include 'SOA/ESB architecture (any)' or require ESB to co-occur with one of {TIBCO, WebMethods, BizTalk, IBM Integration, Mule, Oracle Service Bus, JCAPS, WebSphere}. The bare term in a modern microservices JD ('replacing ESB with Kafka') is borderline-legacy and currently included.

exclusions.reason: PL/SQL fires 268 times (1.4% of the sample) — far higher than other concept members — and is largely a marker of 'Oracle-DB job' rather than 'pre-cloud legacy'. Many snippets are modern Java/cloud roles where Oracle is just the relational store.
exclusions.guard: Down-weight PL/SQL or move it to a separate 'Oracle DB skills' concept. Alternatively, only count it when co-occurring with another legacy marker {Oracle Forms, Oracle Reports, Oracle 10g, Oracle 11g, COBOL, mainframe, PeopleSoft, JD Edwards, Siebel}.

legacy_stack.calibration_recommendations: 1) Compute per-keyword document frequency on a 1% sample of 2024 vs 2026 JDs and sanity-check the top 20 contributors per concept — drop or guard any keyword whose top-document examples are dominantly false positives. 2) For ambiguous tokens (.NET, AD, MQ, RPG, WAS, CVS, Domino, Pascal, VSS), apply co-mention guards described in `exclusions` and re-measure. 3) Build a 'legacy density score' as count of distinct concepts triggered (not raw keyword count) to dampen vocabulary-size bias across concepts. 4) Hold out a 100-JD calibration set, manually label as legacy/modern/mixed, and tune per-concept weights to maximize agreement with human labels (target Cohen's kappa >= 0.7). 5) Track 'modern .NET' (.NET Core, .NET 6/7/8, ASP.NET Core, Blazor, MAUI) as a SEPARATE concept so the legacy vs modern split inside the Microsoft ecosystem can be measured directly. 6) Run keyword density both as raw count per 1000 tokens and as binary presence per JD; report both in the paper because long JDs in regulated industries can inflate raw counts.
legacy_stack.notes: Java 8, Spring (non-Boot), JSP, Servlets, Maven, Perforce, MuleSoft, SOAP, ABAP, LDAP, Kerberos, Hyper-V are all 'borderline' — they appear in both legacy and modern JDs. We include them but recommend treating each as a soft signal that gains weight only when co-occurring with at least one strong signal (mainframe language, .NET Framework, EJB, Active Directory on-prem, BizTalk, etc.). The strongest single-keyword legacy signals are: COBOL, JCL, AS/400, iSeries, z/OS, VB6, Classic ASP, ASP.NET WebForms, .NET Framework, EJB 2, WebLogic, WebSphere, Lotus Notes, PeopleSoft, Siebel, BizTalk, ClearCase, Visual SourceSafe, ColdFusion, Delphi, PowerBuilder, OpenVMS, Hudson. The brief's example of bare '.NET' is INTENTIONALLY downgraded here — see exclusions — because conflating modern and legacy .NET would systematically bias the 2024-vs-2026 trend. Recommend the paper explicitly note this design choice.

context_infrastructure.definition: The informational substrate that enables effective engineering work: human- and machine-readable documentation, observability and telemetry, data-pipeline hygiene, product/business literacy, and cross-functional communication artifacts. Captures language about authoring and maintaining the context (docs, runbooks, dashboards, ADRs, data integrations, stakeholder alignment) on which engineering decisions depend, as opposed to the act of writing code itself.

core_concepts.name: Technical documentation authoring & maintenance
core_concepts.definition: Writing, updating, and curating engineering-facing docs that describe how systems work and why.
core_concepts.regex_notes: Require qualifier (technical/engineering/system/design/code/internal/developer/dev/wiki/readme/knowledge base) or a verb pairing (write/maintain/author/update/curate); bare 'documentation' or 'docs' is too generic.

redefine_proposal.issue: 10.9% headline hit rate is reasonable, but the dominant terms ('technical documentation' 665, 'design documentation' 166, 'system documentation' 137, 'comprehensive documentation' 101, 'clear documentation' 80) include heavy non-SWE contamination (PLC programming, CAD/Visio, mainframe COBOL, Salesforce/ServiceNow boilerplate, automation/firmware engineering). These postings entered the SWE sample but the documentation language is generic engineering boilerplate.

core_concepts.name: Architecture decision records & RFCs
core_concepts.definition: Authoring or reviewing ADRs, RFCs, design proposals, and similar deliberative artifacts.
core_concepts.regex_notes: RFC alone is risky (IETF RFC NNNN cites). Match RFC only when adjacent to 'process', 'document', 'write', 'author', 'review', 'propose', or as 'RFCs' plural; avoid 'RFC \d+' patterns.

redefine_proposal.issue: Concept is mixing engineering decision artifacts (ADRs, RFCs, tech specs, design proposals) with upstream business/product requirements artifacts (BRD, PRD, one-pagers, product requirements document). The latter belong in 'Product & business literacy' or in the process_scaffolding topic; they describe WHAT to build, not the engineering decision about HOW.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Drop BRD, BRDs, PRD, PRDs, product requirements document, one-pagers from this concept. Optionally move them to Product & business literacy (or coordinate with process_scaffolding owner). Tighten definition to 'engineering-authored decision artifacts (ADRs, RFCs, tech specs, design proposals, architecture reviews)'.

core_concepts.name: Runbooks, playbooks, & operational docs
core_concepts.definition: Step-by-step operational artifacts for incidents, on-call, and recurring procedures.
core_concepts.regex_notes: Strong signal terms; few false positives. SOP plural form helps disambiguate from unrelated acronyms.

redefine_proposal.issue: 'playbook'/'playbooks' is dominated by Ansible config-management playbooks (a code artifact for declarative provisioning), not the operational/incident playbooks the concept targets. This injects systematic FPs into an otherwise clean concept.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Add an Ansible exclusion guard for 'playbook' and 'playbooks'. Add 'incident response' and 'PagerDuty' as direct positive signals (see adds).

core_concepts.name: Observability & telemetry stack
core_concepts.definition: Metrics, logs, traces, dashboards, and the systems that produce/consume them.
core_concepts.regex_notes: 'monitor'/'monitoring' is broad — it sometimes means physical hardware monitor or generic supervision. Prefer pairings with logs/metrics/traces/alerts or with named tools. 'metric'/'metrics' alone is fine if observability context is plausible; KPI/business-metric guard handled in exclusions.

redefine_proposal.issue: Mixes high-recall ambiguous terms ('monitoring', 'metrics', 'logs', 'alerts', 'dashboard', 'instrumentation', 'tracing', 'traces') with vendor names that are unambiguous. The single-token entries pick up large numbers of false positives from BI dashboards, business metrics, Workday alerts, hardware instrumentation, and requirements tracing.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Either (a) require an observability-anchor co-occurrence guard for each ambiguous single-token (see guards), or (b) restrict single-token entries to plural/bigram forms that are more discriminative ('dashboards' over 'dashboard', 'distributed tracing' instead of 'tracing'). Vendor list is in good shape — extend with Sentry, Jaeger, Dynatrace, SumoLogic, Fluentd.

core_concepts.name: Service-level reliability targets
core_concepts.definition: Defining and tracking SLOs/SLAs/SLIs and reliability budgets that anchor operational quality.
core_concepts.regex_notes: Acronyms are clean signals in JD context. SLA can sometimes refer to vendor contracts; pairing with 'define', 'meet', 'set', 'monitor', 'track' tightens.

core_concepts.name: Data-pipeline & data-integration hygiene
core_concepts.definition: Maintaining well-integrated, well-documented data flows so downstream consumers and analyses are trustworthy.
core_concepts.regex_notes: 'data pipeline' / 'data integration' / 'data quality' are strong. Bare 'data' is far too broad; require the compound.

core_concepts.name: System understanding & internal knowledge sharing
core_concepts.definition: Activities that build and propagate shared mental models of the system: diagrams, onboarding, knowledge transfer.
core_concepts.regex_notes: 'C4' is short — only match 'C4 model' / 'C4 diagram'.

core_concepts.name: Product & business literacy
core_concepts.definition: Understanding of customers, product strategy, business metrics, and domain context that engineers translate into technical decisions.
core_concepts.regex_notes: 'KPI' / 'OKR' are clean. 'business' / 'customer' / 'product' alone are too generic; require the compound forms listed.

redefine_proposal.issue: Concept rate (0.211) is mostly 'business requirements' (n=1371) and 'business needs' (n=1070), which are generic SWE-posting boilerplate. Stripping them leaves a much smaller, more meaningful product/customer-literacy signal.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Drop or hard-guard the four 'business <noun>' generics (requirements, needs, value, outcomes, impact). Promote tighter signals: 'product sense', 'product mindset', 'product thinking', 'product intuition', 'customer empathy', 'user research', 'domain expertise/knowledge', 'KPIs', 'OKRs'. Expected rate after cleanup: ~6-8%.

core_concepts.name: Cross-functional communication & coordination
core_concepts.definition: Language about working across teams (PM, design, data, marketing, sales, support, legal) and translating between technical and non-technical audiences.
core_concepts.regex_notes: Bare 'stakeholder' / 'communicate' is fluff; only count when in compounds (stakeholder management/alignment/communication, communicate technical concepts, etc.).

redefine_proposal.issue: Anchored on two ultra-high-recall terms ('stakeholders' 20.5%, 'cross-functional' 17.8%) that fire on JD boilerplate in nearly every senior-IC posting. The 36.2% concept hit-rate is largely floor noise rather than signal about cross-functional communication craft. Also has both hyphenated and unhyphenated forms ('cross-functional' vs 'cross functional', 'cross-org' vs 'cross-organizational' vs 'cross-organization' vs 'cross org' vs 'crossfunctional') that collectively over-count.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: (1) Apply verb-anchor guards to 'stakeholders' and 'cross-functional' (see guards). (2) Consolidate hyphenation variants into a single normalized regex per surface form rather than counting each as an independent keyword. (3) Either lower the weight of these two terms or move them to a distinct 'team-shape' concept distinct from 'translation/communication craft' (which is what the concept definition emphasizes).

core_concepts.name: Technical writing craft
core_concepts.definition: Explicit references to writing skill itself — clear written communication as a job skill, beyond generic 'communication'.
core_concepts.regex_notes: Distinct from broad 'communication' fluff. Require the writing/written modifier.

redefine_proposal.issue: 'written communication' (n=1123) is generic resume boilerplate ('excellent verbal and written communication') — does not measure technical-writing craft as defined.
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Hard-guard 'written communication' to require either 'technical' qualifier, 'documentation' nearby, or a written artifact noun. Concept rate after guard will drop to ~2-3%, which is the honest signal.

core_concepts.name: API & interface documentation
core_concepts.definition: Documenting public/internal APIs, schemas, and contracts so consumers (human or agent) can use them correctly.
core_concepts.regex_notes: 'DX' is short — match only when adjacent to 'developer experience'.

redefine_proposal.issue: 'service catalog' is ITSM/ServiceNow terminology, not API documentation; 'DX' is a two-letter token with extreme ambiguity; 'schema documentation' is duplicated across this concept and Data-pipeline & data-integration hygiene (the only within-topic duplicate).
redefine_proposal.suggested_fix: Drop 'service catalog' and 'DX' from this concept. Resolve the 'schema documentation' duplicate by keeping it only in API & interface documentation (the single observed snippet is GraphQL schema documentation, which fits API docs better than data-pipeline hygiene). Add 'Backstage' as a direct positive signal for internal developer portals.

exclusions.reason: Bare 'documentation' is generic — appears in legal/HR/compliance contexts and as boilerplate ('documentation as needed').
exclusions.guard: Require a qualifier (technical/engineering/system/design/code/internal/developer/API/runbook/playbook) or a verb (write/author/maintain/update/curate) within ±3 tokens.

exclusions.reason: Often refers to Google Docs as a tool, or appears as 'docs and tickets' boilerplate.
exclusions.guard: Require qualifier (technical/engineering/system/design/API/dev/developer/internal) or pair with verb.

exclusions.reason: Pure fluff in JDs ('strong communication skills').
exclusions.guard: Only count compound forms: written communication, technical communication, communicate technical concepts, stakeholder communication, executive communication.

exclusions.reason: Generic JD filler.
exclusions.guard: Only count in compounds: stakeholder management/alignment/communication/engagement/updates, align/manage stakeholders, partner with stakeholders.

exclusions.reason: Too broad ('in the context of', 'cultural context').
exclusions.guard: Only count compound forms: business context, product context, system context, customer context, domain context, technical context.

exclusions.reason: May match IETF RFC citations (e.g., 'RFC 7231', 'RFC 2616') rather than internal RFC process.
exclusions.guard: Drop matches of /RFC\s?\d+/. Count 'RFCs' (plural), 'RFC process', 'RFC document', or 'request for comments'.

exclusions.reason: Could be ML eval metrics or business KPIs unrelated to observability telemetry.
exclusions.guard: For observability concept, require co-occurrence with logs/traces/dashboards/alerts/monitoring or named telemetry tool. Otherwise route business-metric mentions to product/business-literacy concept.

exclusions.reason: Used loosely ('monitor team progress', 'monitor budget').
exclusions.guard: Require pairing with system/service/application/infrastructure/production/uptime, or with logs/metrics/traces/alerts.

exclusions.reason: Could mean security alerts, marketing alerts.
exclusions.guard: Pair with monitoring/observability/on-call/PagerDuty/incident, or 'alert rules', 'alerting pipeline'.

exclusions.reason: Most generic word in tech JDs.
exclusions.guard: Only count specific compounds: data pipeline(s), data integration(s), data quality, data lineage, data warehouse, data lake, data catalog, data observability, data contracts, data dictionary, data ingestion, data governance.

exclusions.reason: Sometimes refers to public Wikipedia / external wikis.
exclusions.guard: Bare 'wiki' is acceptable in JDs (overwhelmingly internal); but exclude 'Wikipedia' specifically.

exclusions.reason: May refer to customer-contract SLAs in legal/sales context.
exclusions.guard: Acceptable for this concept either way (still 'service-level' artifact). Optionally tighten with engineering verbs (define/meet/track/monitor).

exclusions.reason: Two-letter acronym collision risk.
exclusions.guard: Match only as the spelled-out 'developer experience' or 'DX' adjacent to 'developer'/'platform'/'tooling'.

exclusions.reason: Generic verb.
exclusions.guard: Match only in compounds: writing skills, writing documentation, writing design documents, technical writing, written communication.

exclusions.reason: Tool names rarely false-positive but may appear as 'familiar with REST/Postman' which is testing-skill, not documentation.
exclusions.guard: Acceptable as documentation signal at this granularity.

exclusions.reason: Could match unrelated alphanumerics.
exclusions.guard: Match only 'C4 model' or 'C4 diagram(s)'.

exclusions.reason: Universal JD boilerplate; almost every posting mentions it.
exclusions.guard: Acceptable but expect saturation; report density rather than presence/absence.

exclusions.pattern: design reviews / design review / architecture review(s)

exclusions.reason: Generic verb across security ('security monitoring'), sysadmin patch management, M&S team activity reports, business case monitoring.
exclusions.guard: Require co-occurrence with one of: alerting, logging, dashboards, observability, telemetry, metrics, traces, Prometheus, Datadog, Grafana, Splunk, ELK, New Relic, CloudWatch, SLO, OpenTelemetry — or limit to compounds 'application monitoring', 'service monitoring', 'system monitoring'.

exclusions.reason: Hits on 'business metrics like CSAT, NPS, NDR', 'quality metrics', 'reliability and performance metrics' — captures product/QA metrics, not telemetry.
exclusions.guard: Require co-occurrence with logs/traces/dashboards/observability/alerts/Prometheus/Datadog/Grafana, OR exclude when preceded by 'business', 'quality', 'KPI', 'success'.

exclusions.reason: Generic ('inspecting application logs', 'logs and reproduction steps in defect tracking'); also 'change logs' / 'audit logs' contexts.
exclusions.guard: Require co-occurrence with metrics OR traces OR observability OR aggregation/centralized/structured.

exclusions.reason: Often appears as a feature of CI/CD or generic 'best practices for logging, monitoring, error handling' boilerplate.
exclusions.guard: Require either a tool (Datadog, Splunk, ELK, Loki) OR a qualifier (centralized, structured, distributed, aggregation) within the same window.

exclusions.reason: Snowflake is a data warehouse product but also generic SQL skill demand — fires on resumes that just list it as a checkbox skill rather than discussing pipeline hygiene.
exclusions.guard: Acceptable as-is for the data-warehousing concept, but document that this is a tool-mention proxy, not a hygiene/quality signal. Consider downweighting versus 'data quality' / 'data validation' for the hygiene interpretation.

exclusions.reason: Same as snowflake — tool checkbox.
exclusions.guard: Same downweighting note; this is technology-stack signal, not hygiene practice signal.

exclusions.reason: Tool-checkbox listing alongside Azure stack.

context_infrastructure.calibration_recommendations: Run the matcher on a stratified sample (~300 postings across 2024 and 2026, mixed seniority) and pull a hit list per concept. (1) Verify that bare-token matches for 'documentation', 'docs', 'data', 'context', 'monitor', 'metrics', 'communication', 'stakeholder', 'RFC' are gated by their compound/guard rules — measure precision before/after the guard. (2) For 'cross-functional' and 'stakeholder' compounds, expect near-100% saturation in senior JDs; consider reporting per-posting *count* (density) rather than binary presence. (3) Decompose role/seniority cells (junior/mid/senior/staff+/EM) before time-trend interpretation: senior+ JDs already mention ADRs, runbooks, and observability heavily, so a temporal shift is meaningful only conditional on level. (4) Spot-check named-tool hits (Datadog, Grafana, dbt, Airflow) against a list of role titles to ensure they are not data-engineer-only artifacts contaminating SWE counts. (5) Track the 'technical writing' concept separately from 'cross-functional communication' — they have different signal profiles and the hypothesis (humans pivot to context curation) predicts the former should rise faster than the latter.
context_infrastructure.notes: Ordering is intentional: artifact-side concepts (docs, ADRs, runbooks, API docs) are listed before behavior-side concepts (system understanding, product literacy, cross-functional communication, technical writing) so that downstream factor analyses can group them. Observability and data-pipeline concepts are kept here (rather than in a separate 'platform' topic) because the hypothesis is about *informational substrate quality*, not platform engineering per se — telemetry and data hygiene are what make the substrate trustworthy. Deliberately excluded: testing/QA (verification topic), code review (verification/process), CI/CD pipelines (process scaffolding), agile ceremonies (process scaffolding), task management/Jira (orchestration/process), prompt engineering and agent management (orchestration). 'Mentorship' is excluded because it overlaps with leadership and is captured elsewhere; only narrowly scoped knowledge-sharing artifacts (onboarding docs, tech talks, internal blog) are included here.
